Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster. Engineering Lead for Fleet Management As the Engineering Lead for Fleet Management, you will ensure our merchants can deploy and scale their in-person payment infrastructure with zero friction. You own the technical strategy for our global supply chain, automating the entire lifecycle of a terminal: factory ordering, global distribution, replacement, decommissioning, and reporting. Intuitive and self-service tools are the key to empowering merchants to manage their own stores and terminal fleets independently. You will be driven to create a simple, seamless, and automated experience from a complex logistics operation. You will lead the development of the configuration of our terminals - handling everything from merchant settings and dynamic content to payment kernel and SoftPOS configurations. The integrity of our Android ecosystem also falls within scope, overseeing the secure signing and deployment of apps and certificates. What You'll Do As the Engineering Lead, reporting to the SVP of Payments and collaborating with peers globally, your focus will be: Provide clear guidance, purpose, and mission for engineering teams. Create an environment that fosters continuous innovation. Focused on quality, have a high attention to detail and automation. Being driven by data and wanting to know what’s happening on our merchant’s terminals is a must. Build a best-in-class team; developing and hiring great people with the right skills to deliver. Curious, approachable and desire to work in a highly collaborative manner - both internal and external. Exceptional at prioritization, putting our merchant and business needs ahead of personal ambition. Accountable for the execution of key projects, ensuring timely delivery, tracking progress, removing blockers, and assisting with planning and prioritization as needed. Driven by simplicity, standards and reuse. Global Partner Engineering Manager, Payments Adyen is seeking a Global Manager for the Payments Partner Engineering team. This role requires a leader with a solid technical foundation, deep operational instincts, and a proven ability to build and develop high-performing teams. You will be responsible for the health of Adyen's payment partner integrations — spanning global card schemes, banks, wallets, local and alternative payment methods — and for evolving the Partner Engineering function to reduce reactive operational work to proactively addressing partner integration health. As the leader of the global Payments Partner Eng team, you will manage a team of Payment Partner Engineers who serve as Adyen's technical point of accountability for payment partner integrations. Your goal will be to drive integration reliability and performance across Adyen's global payment method portfolio, while building the tools, processes, and capabilities that allow the team to scale. This position requires a blend of leadership, technical and payments domain depth, strategic thinking, and the ability to influence both internal stakeholders and external payment partners. This role is critical in ensuring Adyen's payment partner integrations meet the reliability and performance standards that underpin our merchant value proposition. The ideal candidate has led technical, cross-functional teams in complex environments, understands payments and API-based integrations, and brings a proactive, high-agency mindset to building a team and function from a still-early stage. What you'll do: Lead, mentor, and coach a global team of Payment Partner Engineers, fostering their growth and ensuring they are effective technical counterparts to Adyen's payment partners. Define and drive a vision for the Paments Partner Engineering function — shifting the team's center of gravity from reactive issue management toward proactive partnership, performance ownership, and operational leverage. Own integration health across Adyen's payment method portfolio, with accountability for reliability, performance (auth rates, settlement, disputes, etc.), and the team’s service model to internal and external stakeholders. Drive strategic architectural recommendations to ensure partners optimally integrate with Adyen, maximizing value for Adyen’s merchant customers. P

As Senior Service Designer for Platform Engineering, you will improve the end-to-end developer experience across internal tools, workflows, and platform capabilities. You will act as the bridge between high-level strategy and tactical execution, making Platform Engineering feel and function like a single, cohesive experience rather than ~100 disconnected products. You will start with the Developer Center — the central developer platform and launch pad for everything else — then expand to the journey across the wider developer ecosystem, which today is fragmented and inconsistent. You will work on both improving what exists and shaping what comes next. Service design as a coordinated practice does not yet exist at Adyen. You are not inheriting a playbook — you are writing it, and socializing it across PX as the example of what good looks like.     Real scope, real impact. Your users are ~1,500 engineers. The frictions you remove and the journeys you connect translate directly into how fast Adyen can build and ship.     A seat where decisions are made. You will influence and co-create roadmaps and priorities with engineering and product leadership — not just respond to them.     Room to grow the craft. You will teach and share service design across the PX practice, helping other designers adopt these methods and creating a multiplier effect well beyond your own projects. description: Software engineers and AI agents alike suffer from the same problem: finding that one person or place that will answer their tough, specific question. Many solutions promise to solve this with similarity search in vector databases. Unfortunately, finding the answer is often a puzzle with pieces to be collected across a myriad of contradictory sources and cannot be solved without surgical search and careful reasoning.  Spectrum collects data from an organization’s code, docs, and issues, and organizes knowledge in a unified ontology that AI agents can efficiently search through and reason over. We aim to revolutionize the semantic layer space for software-building organizations and move beyond specs that fall out of sync with code, introducing a living spec – one that’s extracted from the whole system and used to keep it aligned. Spectrum is meant to be the single source of truth for all product and architectural knowledge. Spectrum is a resident of JetBrains' startup incubator, with startup speed and autonomy, and backed by 25 years of developer tooling expertise. We are looking for a top-class ML Engineer who will help us shape the future of software development. You will own our AI and ML engineering stack and help define the research agenda for our team. Your technical vision and design decisions will directly shape the product and determine its success. Your responsibilities will include: Designing and building the ML/LLM solution for data ingestion, knowledge extraction, retrieval, and subsequent reasoning. Creating the datasets, metrics, and pipelines that drive measurable improvements across the system. Architecting and improving agents for context retrieval, knowledge extraction, and data alignment, which includes prompt engineering, model selection, and inference optimization. Establishing MLOps practices, including orchestration, observability, and experiment tracking. Collaborating with the engineering team on system design and with JetBrains Research on the research agenda. Defining hiring criteria, growing the ML team, and shaping the ML team culture. We expect you to have: A proven track record as an ML/AI Lead. At least five years of experience in ML/AI systems, with at least two years focused on LLMs and generative AI. A deep understanding of the LLM ecosystem, including model architectures and fine-tuning approaches. Hands-on experience with: Prompt engineering and LLM pipeline design, including evaluation. Agentic frameworks such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, LangSmith, smolagents, or an equivalent. Vector databases and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) patterns. Deploying and scaling LLM-powered applications using APIs (e.g. OpenAI or Anthropic) or open-source models. Strong Python skills – Kotlin knowledge would be a plus. Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to diverse audiences. Proficiency in English, both written and verbal.  Our ideal candidate would have: Experie

The recent trade crisis—sparked by Trump-era tariffs—has made customs the single most complex challenge for global businesses. For many, it’s the difference between keeping supply chains moving or grinding them to a halt. That’s where we come in. The Customs Technology team at Flexport is leading the charge. We are building the systems that help entrepreneurs and global brands navigate complexity, stay compliant, and keep the world’s goods flowing. The opportunity:  Customs brokerage is one of the largest, most under-innovated opportunities in logistics. Legacy customs brokers are stuck with outdated systems and manual processes—they simply can’t adapt fast enough. Flexport is growing fast because we’re different. We are the only big tech company in the space, and the only freight forwarder that got founded after the launch of Netscape. The job in short As a Senior System Engineer, you will join the crossfunctional team (System, Security and Software engineers) owning our Integration Platform as a Service (IPaaS), “Grand Central”. Our platform enables our customers (banks, financial institutions) to build and run integrations in a reliable and efficient way. In this role you will drive its uptime, automation, performance, security, and long-term architecture. Your technical and operational experience will be essential in bringing this platform to the next level. You won't just contribute—you will define and drive the technical roadmap to scale our services and shape the future of our IPaaS. We are looking for a seasoned, hands-on engineer who is both a strategic thinker and a “doer”.  We expect you to bring deep technical expertise in at least 3 domains: Cloud (Azure), Kubernetes, Istio, Networking, Infrastructure as Code, Automation and Observability. You excel at troubleshooting - diagnosing and resolving the most complex systemic issues, while equally enjoying the challenge of building resilient, scalable systems from the ground up . What You'll Do Own the Architecture: Design, build, and drive the evolution of our Integration Platform running in Azure, ensuring it meets standards of scalability, performance, and resilience. Guarantee Platform Integrity: Take ownership of the availability and reliability of the platform. Lead Incident Response: Participate in on-call rotations, serving as the final point of escalation for complex issues to ensure maximum reliability and availability. Drive Operational Excellence: Proactively identify and eliminate operational inefficiencies through automation, monitoring, and cost management initiatives. Mentor and Elevate: Foster a culture of continuous learning and technical excellence by mentoring team members, sharing expertise, and leading by example. Who You Are Core Qualifications: Extensive Experience: 6+ years of experience in system engineering, with a proven track record of designing, implementing, and running highly-available workloads in enterprise cloud environments. Autonomous & Proactive: You work independently, take initiative, and can lead complex projects from concept to completion with minimal oversight. Mentorship Mindset: You combine deep technical expertise with a passion for mentorship, providing peers with hands-on guidance and collaborative problem-solving. Automation-First Philosophy: Your primary goal is to make every process predictable, repeatable, and fully automated. Security Expertise: You possess a deep and practical understanding of cloud infrastructure and software security principles. Technical Proficiencies: Expertise in public cloud: Azure Cloud ( preferred) - practical hands on experience in running and building production infrastructure Infrastructure as Code: Expert-level user of Terraform or OpenTofu. Kubernetes Ecosystem: Proficient with Helm, Kubernetes, ArgoCD, and Kustomiz

description: A World-Changing Company Palantir builds the world’s leading software for data-driven decisions and operations. By bringing the right data to the people who need it, our platforms empower our partners to develop lifesaving drugs, forecast supply chain disruptions, locate missing children, and more. The Role Forward Deployed Software Engineers (FDSEs) work directly with customers to quickly understand their greatest problems and design and implement solutions to use data against them. Our customers rely on Palantir’s platforms for some of their most critical operations, and projects often start with an open ended question like “Why are we delaying so many flights?” or “How can we better identify instances of money laundering?” As an FDSE, you’ll apply your problem solving ability, creativity, and technical skills to help organizations use their data to drive a real impact in the world. You’ll have the opportunity to gain rare insight into and contribute to some of the world’s most important industries and institutions. As an FDSE, your responsibilities look similar to those of a startup CTO: you’ll work in small teams with minimal supervision and own end-to-end execution of high stakes projects. Your day might span discussing architecture with fellow engineers, wrangling massive-scale data, coding a custom web app, speaking with customer executives, or establishing strategy for your team. Whether you aspire to be an entrepreneur or an engineering leader, we believe Palantir is the best place — with the best colleagues — to learn how. You’ll learn how to unpack a problem and understand the costs and consequences of its solution. You’ll learn new technologies and languages, and even develop them yourself. You’ll work autonomously and make decisions independently, within a community that will support and challenge you as you grow and develop.

description: Flow Traders is looking for a Senior Research Engineer, successful candidate will be relocated to our Hong Kong Office. This is a unique opportunity to join a leading proprietary trading firm with an entrepreneurial and innovative culture at the heart of its business. We value quick-witted, creative minds and challenge them to make full use of their capacities.  As a Senior Research Engineer, you will be responsible for helping to lead the development of our trading model research framework and using it to conduct research to develop models for trading in production. You'll expand the framework to become global standard way of training, consuming, combining, and transforming any data source in a data-driven systematic way. You will then partner with Quantitative Researchers to build the trading models themselves. What You Will Do Help to lead the development and global rollout of our research framework for defining and training models through various optimization procedures (supervised learning, backtesting etc.), as well as its integration with our platform for deploying and running those models in production Partner with Quantitative Researchers to conduct research: test hypotheses and tune/develop data-driven systematic trading strategies and alpha signals What You Need to Succeed Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in Machine Learning, Statistics, Physics, Computer Science or similar 8+ years of hands-on experience MLOps, Research Engineering, or ML Research A strong background in mathematics and statistics Strong proficiency in programming languages such as Python, with experience in libraries like numpy, pytorch, polars, pandas, and ray. We’re reimagining car insurance—and we’re looking for a senior backend engineer to help lead the charge. As a part of our Telematics team, you’ll take a leading role in shaping the platform behind Lemonade Car. You'll design and build systems that turn raw sensor data into real-time insights, smarter pricing, and safer driving experiences, making an immediate, real-world impact. We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work. Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools. In this role you’ll Own platform end to end: Lead the design, implementation, and evolution of our sensor-agnostic telematics platform, with focus on scalability, performance, and quality. Build big: Design, implement and optimize our telematics data pipelines processing vast volumes of customer data from various sources such as mobile phones, plug-in tracking devices, or connected cars. Solve hard problems: Partner with teams across the company to tackle complex challenges like pricing fairness, fraud prevention, and risk mitigation. Integrate at scale: Work with car makers (OEMs), driving data vendors and fulfillment providers to deliver a seamless, delightful experience to our customers. Unlock the data: Model and expose telematics data for a wide range of use cases – from real-time decision making to data science and analytics. Ship modern infrastructure: Build microservices in TypeScript (NestJS), running on Kubernetes, in a cloud-native environment. Move fast with great tooling: Use our in-house DevX and AI tools to streamline everything from design to development, deploying, and running services in production. Lead by example: Mentor and coach fellow engineers, raising the bar for technical excellence across the company. What you’ll need 7+ years of backend engineering experience, building production systems at scale. BSc in Computer Science (or equivalent experience) Deep knowledge of modern backend frameworks, tools, and architecture Proven experience building high-throughput, real-time data streaming systems, using Kafka, SQS, or similar technologies Experience with AWS or other public cloud providers Familiarity with data engineering and orchestration tools (Athena, Kinesis, Glue, Airflow) Bonus points for experience in telematics, IoT, or sensor-driven platforms, as well as meaningful contributions to open-source projects Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

We're looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to help build the core systems that power how Lemonade prices insurance for millions of customers. You'll join our Pricing Team in Amsterdam, working on everything from our homegrown domain-specific language to AI-powered research pipelines that push the boundaries of actuarial science. Team Context Our Pricing Team operates at the intersection of complex mathematics and high-performance engineering. We've built our own DSL in TypeScript for actuarial calculations, compiled to Rust for production performance, and increasingly leverage AI to discover new pricing patterns. You'll work alongside actuaries who understand the logic of risk, but you'll be the one designing the technical architecture that makes their insights scale. We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. In this role you'll Design and build core rating services that calculate prices for insurance products used by millions Architect complex systems spanning TypeScript, Rust, and Python across different problem domains Build AI-powered research pipelines that help actuaries discover new pricing insights Create SDKs and code patterns that other teams can build on top of Optimize performance-critical systems where milliseconds matter for customer experience Collaborate with actuaries to translate complex mathematical models into scalable code Lead technical decisions about system architecture, interfaces, and design patterns What you'll need Adaptability, drive, and an efficiency mindset — we believe these matter most in human-AI collaboration Deep experience with system design and architectural decision-making, not just API development Strong proficiency in one of backend languages with ability to quickly pick up new ones (our stack includes TypeScript, Rust, and Python) Experience building performance-optimized systems where technical choices directly impact business outcomes Track record of designing complex technical systems from scratch, including interfaces and abstractions Comfort working with heavy Kubernetes deployments and cloud-native architectures would be nice to have Educational degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI — a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture

Who are we? At Aquablu we’re disrupting the marketplace with a revolutionary (flavored) water system. Brought to life in 2018, our founders Marnix and Marc promised a healthy but tasty alternative to soft drinks. Today, our water is tapped in almost 16 countries. And we continue to make waves by teaming up with industry leaders like Heineken, Adyen and Marriott Hotels. Join us in our mission to save one billion plastic bottles by 2030! “That might sound really ambitious, but our team is part of an inspiring movement in an industry that’s thirsty for change.” - Marc, Co-founder of Aquablu About the Job: How fast our product builders go from idea to something real in users' hands is how our digital products win. You build the engine that makes that speed possible (and safe) by default. You build the platform underneath everyone who ships: the prototyping environment, the deploy pipelines, the test and eval harnesses, the AI tooling, and the runtime our AI agents and automated workflows run on. When a builder has an idea on Monday and it's in front of users on Friday, your platform is why — and why everyone can trust it works. Every hour you save the team compounds across every product we build. This is a deeply hands-on senior engineering role with wide autonomy. You scope, decide, and deliver the platform yourself — you're the domain expert everyone who ships comes to, and the standards you set become how the whole team works. You're also the quality owner for the digital org: the testing strategy, the eval strategy, and the bar itself sit with you, encoded in infrastructure rather than enforced by meetings. You're in the code every day, building real infrastructure with AI as your primary collaborator. One day you're cutting a deploy pipeline from hours to minutes; the next you're building the eval harness so a builder can trust a feature behaves before it ever reaches a user; the day after you're putting guardrails and monitoring around an agent that runs company workflows unsupervised. You own the rails; the builders and engineers own what runs on them. Your job is that everything on those rails ships fast, behaves predictably, and stays affordable. If building the thing that makes a whole team faster excites you more than shipping any single feature: this is the role for you. This role reports into our Head of Digital Product, and is part of Aquablu's Software & Intelligence Engineering team. It’s a full-time position in our office in Amsterdam, Houthavens and office presence is 4 days minimum per week.

🌊 Who are we? We're building the world's largest hydration platform to replace a $1.5 trillion industry controlled by Coca-Cola and Pepsi — companies selling sugar water in plastic. Their model is stuck in the past. We're changing that, from the workplace. In 2025: #2 fastest growing tech company in the Netherlands (Deloitte Fast50). #30 fastest growing in Europe (Sifted250). We're an AI-native company — AI is how we build, not an afterthought. About the role Aquablu builds connected water dispensers that sit at the intersection of industrial hardware, embedded software, and IoT, deployed across hundreds of sites and scaling fast. We're at the stage where the engineering organisation needs to grow up: better processes, higher output, without losing the speed that got us here. As Engineering Manager, you own that. You lead the three engineering pillars: Hardware Engineering, Embedded Software, and Process Development & Industrialization. That means taking products from concept to manufacturing release, building the team and processes to do it repeatedly, and setting a technical bar that attracts and retains great engineers. You report to the Head of Physical Product. Engineering and Product work tightly together here. You'll have a real voice in what gets built and how, with the autonomy to make technical calls and own their outcomes. What you'll own Lead three engineering pillars : Hardware Engineering, Embedded Software, and Industrialization. You push direction, remove blockers, and hold the bar. Drive DFM and industrialization decisions : material selection, tolerances, cost targets, production scalability. You've done this before and know what it takes to get hardware that's designed for real-world manufacturing. Own the full NPI process : from prototype through production release. No handoffs to hide behind; you're accountable end to end. Build the team : hire well, develop people, establish engineering standards. You leave the team stronger than you found it. Improve how engineering works : design reviews, testing protocols, documentation, ways of working. You build the system, not just the product.

job_title: Client Service Lead - AI Engineering Competence Center
description: Jij verkoopt geen AI. Jij brengt het tot stand. Dit is geen specialistrol. Dit is een commerciële rol voor iemand die begrijpt wat AI engineering voor organisaties betekent, en die van iO dé partner maakt die dat niet alleen belooft maar ook levert. Bij iO bouwen we met het AI Engineering Competence Center (AIECC) aan een landelijke capability die AI-oplossingen echt neerzet bij onze klanten. Geen experimenten die op de plank blijven liggen, maar schaalbare AI-oplossingen met workflows en agents die in productie draaien en bedrijfsprocessen fundamenteel veranderen. Voor organisaties als Worldline, SBB, Wageningen Universiteit en ETEX doen we dit al. Jij zorgt dat we dit ook bij de volgende twintig doen. Wat je doet Je combineert new business met het uitbouwen van bestaande klantrelaties, met AI als rode draad door alles heen. New business Je opent nieuwe deuren voor het AIECC. Deels via bestaande klantrelaties van iO waar AI nog niet of nauwelijks aan bod is geweest, deels via partnerkanalen zoals AWS en Contentstack waarmee iO samenwerkt. Je begrijpt waar budgetten en vraagstukken samenkomen, en weet hoe je van een eerste gesprek een opdracht maakt. De dealgroottes variëren breed, van €20K tot €200K, passend bij de fase waarin de markt zich nu bevindt. Jij navigeert dat spectrum moeiteloos. Accountgroei Je voert regie over een portfolio bestaande klanten en bent eindverantwoordelijk voor relatie én commercieel resultaat. Je ontwikkelt accountplannen met heldere AI-groeidoelstellingen, signaleert cross- en upsellkansen en vertaalt die naar concrete proposities. Je organiseert kwartaalmeetings, bewaakt kwaliteit en klanttevredenheid, en zorgt voor tijdige contractverlengingen en indexaties. Verbinden van commercie en delivery Het AIECC is een landelijk initiatief met experts verspreid over onze vijf Nederlandse campussen. Delivery en kennisopbouw zitten qua focus in Eindhoven en Den Bosch, het consultancy-deel wordt mede gedragen vanuit Amsterdam en Utrecht. Jij bent de brug tussen wat de klant wil bereiken en wat ons team technisch en strategisch kan leveren. Je spreekt de taal van AI-engineers én die van de business. Dankzij je kennis van AI engineering, LLM-toepassingen en AI-architectuur kun je inhoudelijk sparren, teams uitdagen, klanten adviseren en AI-oplossingen succesvol naar productie brengen." Thought leadership Je bent een ambassadeur voor AI binnen én buiten iO. Je helpt klanten de mogelijkheden te zien, bouwt aan langdurige relaties en versterkt iO's positie als toonaangevende AI-partner. Je volgt de nieuwste ontwikkelingen, deelt dat actief op LinkedIn en draagt bij aan iO's zichtbaarheid in het AI-landschap.

Modern digital products run on platforms: services, APIs, integrations, and the cloud-native foundation that ties them together. At Hypersolid, you build across that whole picture, frontend to backend to the integrations in between, in composable environments where services are independent and everything talks through APIs. We call this role Platform Engineer rather than fullstack on purpose. Working agentically changes what "fullstack" means: you move further across the stack, faster, and your job is to direct that work and judge it, not just type it. As a Platform Engineering intern, you work inside that reality from day one. What you'll do You join a platform team and own real tasks on real projects. Not isolated exercises. Not a sandbox. Together with your mentor, you set learning goals and shape an internship that builds genuine depth across development: building features end-to-end, designing APIs, and wiring services together in the cloud. Graduation internship (from September 2026): you research a platform-engineering challenge, build a prototype or proof of concept, and present your findings as your graduation project. Working internship: in some cases possible, contributing directly to live projects. What the work looks like You work across the stack, from the services and APIs to the cloud they run on. Agentic tools get you to working code fast, but the distance from generated code to something reliable and production-ready is where the real work lives, and that judgment stays yours. You direct the agent, then review what it produces: you run your own tests, verify behaviour in the target environment, and confirm the work meets requirements before it goes to review. You never accept generated output uncritically. Seniors challenge you. Ownership is yours. The specific stack matters less than the mindset. We're not looking for someone who's mastered one technology, we're looking for someone who picks things up fast, thinks in outcomes, and uses every tool available to get there. Who you are You're studying software development, computer science, or a related field. More importantly: agentic AI isn't a topic you follow from a distance, you're deep in it. You build things with it, experiment outside class, and have opinions about what works and what doesn't. You're the person your friends ask which tool to use. You take ownership, surface problems early, and care about the quality of what you ship. Bring something you built. It tells us more than your CV.

Joining Hypersolid as a Full-Stack Developer means taking ownership of features from concept through deployment. You build robust, scalable applications by connecting polished frontend experiences to reliable backend services. You contribute to creating seamless, end-to-end digital products, often enhanced with AI-driven features. What are you going to do? You work closely with designers, developers, and engineers to translate ideas into working solutions. You take responsibility for your work, think about improvements, and contribute to the overall quality of the codebase and product. As you grow in the role, you become increasingly involved in technical decisions and help improve how we build and deliver software. Additional tasks: Develop and maintain full-stack applications using our core stack. Build responsive and performant user interfaces with React and Next.js. Develop and manage APIs and server-side logic using Node.js. Contribute to AI-powered features using LLMs and external AI services. Collaborate with infrastructure engineers to deploy and maintain cloud infrastructure on AWS. Ensure performance, scalability, and reliability of applications. Contribute to improving development workflows, tooling, and code quality. Who are we looking for? We are looking for someone who is curious, collaborative, and takes ownership of their work. You enjoy building high-quality digital products and are motivated to keep learning and improving your skills. You have a good eye for both code quality and user experience, and you are comfortable working in a team where sharing ideas and feedback is part of the process. As a person, you bring: A strong sense of ownership and responsibility for your work. A collaborative mindset and clear communication skills. A proactive attitude and willingness to learn new technologies. An interest in AI and modern development workflows. A good sense of design and usability. The ability to balance quality, performance, and pragmatism. Requirements Completed HBO (Bachelor) education or equivalent relevant work experience. 5+ years of experience as a Full Stack Developer or similar role. Experience with React, Next.js, and modern frontend tooling. Experience with Node.js and building APIs. Experience with headless CMS platforms such as Sanity, Contentful, DatoCMS, or Strapi. Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS. Familiarity with AI/ML APIs (e.g. OpenAI, Hugging Face, Anthropic) is a plus. Experience working with Figma and design systems is a plus. Knowledge of SQL and/or NoSQL databases. You speak and write English fluently.

Brief info about Vinted  Our mission is to make second-hand the first choice, and we're looking for people who want to help us get there. Every day, we work together to help our members buy and sell pre-loved clothing and lifestyle items, giving each piece a second life – or even a third. The Vinted Group is made up of three business units that support this mission: Vinted Marketplace is Europe’s leading platform for second-hand fashion and a go-to destination for all kinds of pre-loved items, with a growing range of categories. Seamlessly integrated into the Vinted app, it helps keep every transaction safe, efficient, and easy for our members.
